metadata.description: CS Knowledge Base for Chuck CS Assistant — Customer Success specific knowledge for Informativ CS team

changelog.change: Added email_drafting_standards category with Marketing-approved email rules (Christina Wofford review)

product_architecture.label: Product Architecture
product_architecture.description: Informativ product portfolio, capabilities, and bundling

chunks.title: Informativ Product Portfolio Overview
chunks.content:
Informativ is a SaaS platform serving automotive dealerships with credit, compliance, fraud prevention, and customer insights solutions.

Core Products:
- CBC (Credit Bureau Connection): Hard and soft credit pulls from Equifax, Experian, TransUnion. Supports VantageScore and FICO models. API and portal access.
- DSGSS (Compliance Platform): Digital deal jacket creation, driver's license scanning, ID verification (eVerify), Red Flag compliance, adverse action notices, risk-based pricing notices, OFAC checks, e-signatures, 5-year dead deal storage.
- SmartPencil: Credit-qualified payment engine. Combines credit profile with lender programs to structure optimized deals. Maximizes dealer profitability while ensuring fundability.
- Informativ Payments: Real payment offers (not estimates) across ads, websites, marketplaces. Smart engine optimizes for margin and affordability.
- CreditDriver: Website soft pull widget for online pre-qualification. Generates leads with credit data attached.
- eZ Form Fill: Automated form population from credit data.
- TurboPass (Verified Stips): Automated stipulation collection and verification. Fraud detection via document authentication.
- eClear: Document fraud detection and prevention.

Product Bundling: Products are bundled into tiered packages (Foundation, Detect, Protect, Control) plus standalone Total Solutions/Elevate/Enrich configurations.

chunks.title: Tiered Package Definitions
chunks.content:
Informativ offers 4 core product tiers, each building on the previous:

FOUNDATION: Credit reports from any supported system (RouteOne/DealerTrack API). Paperless sales process. Website credit application for hard and soft pulls. Flexible refillable credit bank. Simple flat-rate pricing.

DETECT: Everything in Foundation PLUS: Red Flag compliance hard stop alerts. Driver's license scan for ID verification. Synthetic fraud detection with out-of-wallet questions. Real-time fraud alerts. Verified automated encrypted stips collection. Risk-based pricing notices. OFAC check. 5-year dead deal jacket storage. Proactive compliant workflows with e-signatures.

PROTECT: Everything in Detect PLUS: Additional fraud detection to enhance compliance. Paperless credit and privacy notice documents. Digital deal jacket creation and secure storage. Adverse action notices (electronic and mail house). Seamless soft pull workflows to reduce compliance burden. Tech to minimize customer friction and improve buying experience.

CONTROL: Everything in Protect PLUS: Showroom credit controls on all in-store and remote deals. Credit optimization to control spend. Customer insights dashboard to identify buying potential and improve closing rates. Speed up the desk process. Proactive consistent compliant workflows with e-signatures.

chunks.title: Total Solutions Use Case Flow
chunks.content:
Total Solutions combines Credit + Compliance + SmartPencil into a unified workflow:

Lead Sources → DSGSS Funnel:
- CBC Web Lead, CreditDriver, Digital Retail, Walk-In
- All leads funnel into DSGSS regardless of origin
- CreditDriver and CBC Web App can bypass DSGSS and send direct to CRM

Workflow Steps:
1. Digital Deal Jacket (DDJ) Created — started by web app lead, CreditDriver lead, digital retail lead, or walk-in DL scan
2. Driver License Scan — ID verification, permissible purpose captured
3. Credit & Privacy Notice Documents — signed digitally
4. CBC Hard Pull — Red Flags, OOW Questions, Adverse Action, Risk-Based Pricing disclosure
5. STIPS Collection — capture of all documents during sales process
6. E-Contracting — forms added (personal/business credit apps, credit apps over 3 pages)
7. Send DDJ to RouteOne or DealerTrack

SmartPencil Integration: After soft pull, SmartPencil uses credit profile + lender programs to generate optimized payment structures. Desk managers see real payments, not estimates.

chunks.title: SmartPencil Architecture & Capabilities
chunks.content:
SmartPencil is Informativ's credit-qualified payment engine — the flagship innovation combining credit + payments.

How It Works:
1. Leverages customer's credit profile (from soft or hard pull)
2. Aligns with lending institutions' lending profiles and programs
3. Structures upfront deals that maximize profitability
4. Accelerates approval process

Key Features:
- Credit-qualified payments (not estimates — real, transactable offers)
- Seamless integration into Informativ platform
- Maintains accuracy and compliance focus
- Speed optimization for faster deal closing
- Supports loan and lease structures
- Quick Quote functionality
- LMS push capability
- Desk Manager mode for F&I professionals

Dealer Benefits:
- Higher engagement from first touch to final pencil
- More conversions through reduced friction
- Increased sales efficiency (faster funnel = less time at desk)
- Maximize profit through smart deal structuring
- Automatically selects best lender, rate, and incentive per shopper
- Builds consumer trust with real payment information

Training: 6 SmartPencil buttonology modules available (Getting Ready, Buttonology, Loan/Lease, Recommendations, Quick Quote, LMS Push).

pricing_tiers.label: Pricing & Tier Economics
pricing_tiers.description: Pricing governance, tier economics, margin targets

chunks.title: Pricing Governance & Approval Thresholds
chunks.content:
Informativ Pricing Governance — Effective March 2026

Pricing Approval Thresholds by Package Tier:
- Protect: Target GM% 47.5%, Manager Approval 42.5%, Deal Desk Floor 39.5%
- Enrich: Target GM% 52.5%, Manager Approval 47.5%, Deal Desk Floor 44.5%
- Elevate: Target GM% 60.0%, Manager Approval 55.0%, Deal Desk Floor 52.0%
- Control: Target GM% 50.0%, Manager Approval 45.0%, Deal Desk Floor 42.0%

How Approval Works:
- At or above Target GM%: Standard pricing, no approval needed
- Between Target and Manager Threshold: Manager approval required
- Below Manager Threshold: Deal Desk review required (will optimize pricing upward)
- No deal priced below tier-specific floor

Key Principle: Higher-value packages carry higher margin expectations. Anchor pricing conversations on VALUE delivered, not cost. Use the Informativ Method: teach the dealer what they're losing today, tailor the package to their business model, take control of the pricing conversation by framing margin protection as a benefit.

chunks.title: Tier Economics & Revenue Model
chunks.content:
Revenue Model by Customer Type:

TIERED/FLAT RATE CUSTOMERS (CSA accounts):
- Fixed monthly subscription fee based on tier (Protect/Enrich/Elevate/Control)
- Includes CPQ allotment (soft pulls + hard pulls per month)
- Overages billed when usage exceeds allotment
- Revenue = Subscription MRR + Transaction MRR + Overage MRR + Invoiced MRR + Credit Memo MRR
- Net MRR = Sum of all MRR components
- Target margin varies by tier (39.5% floor to 60% target)

CREDIT/TRANSACTIONAL CUSTOMERS (CSM accounts):
- Per-pull pricing (each credit pull billed individually)
- No subscription — pure transaction revenue
- Revenue scales with pull volume
- Target margin: 27%+ healthy, 16-26% watch, 0-15% critical
- Lower margin expectations but higher volume sensitivity

Key CS Insight: Tiered customers have predictable revenue (subscription) but usage must stay within allotment. Credit customers have variable revenue tied to dealer activity — volume drops signal churn risk.

bureau_economics.label: Bureau Economics
bureau_economics.description: Bureau cost structure, rates, optimization strategies

chunks.title: Bureau Cost Structure & Rates
chunks.content:
Bureau COGS Rate Card (current rates from cogs-rates.json):

Hard Pulls:
- Equifax Hard Pull (EFX_HP): $7.06/pull
- Experian Hard Pull (EXP_HP): $5.33/pull
- TransUnion Hard Pull (TU_HP): $7.48/pull

Soft Pulls:
- Equifax Soft Pull (EFX_SP): $7.06/pull
- Experian Soft Pull (EXP_SP): $4.78/pull
- TransUnion Soft Pull (TU_SP): $7.59/pull

Additional Product COGS:
- Synthetic ID Verification: $0.16/check
- Compliance Suite (DSGSS): $80/month
- Informativ Payments Base: $208.25/month
- SmartPencils: $0.5/use
- DL Fraud Check: $120/month
- DealerTrack Fee: $150/month

Margin Calculation: GM% = (Net MRR - Total COGS) / Net MRR × 100
Total COGS = Bureau pull costs + Product COGS + Subcode fees

chunks.title: Bureau Optimization Strategies
chunks.content:
Three primary bureau optimization scenarios for CS conversations:

SCENARIO 1: 3-Bureau to 2-Bureau Reduction
- Customer pulling from all 3 bureaus (EQ + EX + TU) on every transaction
- Opportunity: Reduce to 2 bureaus for soft pulls while maintaining hard pull coverage
- Savings: Eliminate ~33% of soft pull COGS
- Talk track: "Most lenders only require 1-2 bureau reports. We can optimize your bureau configuration to reduce costs without impacting your lending relationships."

SCENARIO 2: SmartPull Activation
- Customer not using SmartPull (intelligent bureau routing)
- SmartPull automatically selects optimal bureau per transaction based on lender requirements
- Reduces unnecessary duplicate pulls
- Talk track: "SmartPull intelligently routes each credit request to the optimal bureau, eliminating redundant pulls and reducing your per-transaction costs."

SCENARIO 3: Score-Only Pulls
- Customer running full credit reports when only scores are needed for pre-qualification
- Score-only pulls cost significantly less than full reports
- Appropriate for initial customer screening before full pull
- Talk track: "For initial customer engagement, a score-only pull gives you the credit insight you need at a fraction of the cost of a full report."

CS Action: Review bureau pull patterns in CI Dashboard Bureau Optimization tab. Flag customers with >2 bureau soft pull average for optimization conversation.

chunks.title: Health Score Calculation (5 Dimensions)
chunks.content:
Customer Health Score Model — Composite score 0-100, weighted across 5 dimensions:

1. MARGIN HEALTH (30% weight):
   - Measures current gross margin percentage relative to thresholds
   - Two-track system: Tiered customers vs Credit customers have different scales
   - Tiered: Healthy ≥40%, Watch 20-40%, Critical 0-20%, Negative <0%
   - Credit: Healthy ≥27%, Watch 16-26%, Critical 0-15%, Negative <0%
   - Includes margin velocity (slope over recent months)

2. BUREAU EFFICIENCY (15% weight):
   - Ratio of actual bureau usage vs expected/efficient usage
   - Flags over-pulling (3-bureau when 2 suffices) and under-utilization
   - SmartPull adoption factor
   - Bureau mix optimization score

3. PRODUCT UTILIZATION (20% weight):
   - For tiered customers: CPQ usage vs allotment (soft pulls + hard pulls)
   - For credit customers: Pull volume trends vs historical baseline
   - Feature adoption score (guardrails, SmartPull, compliance features)
   - Under-utilization (<50%) signals disengagement risk
   - Over-utilization (>100%) signals potential overage billing issues

4. GROWTH TRAJECTORY (20% weight):
   - MRR trend over T12 (trailing 12 months)
   - Revenue velocity (positive/negative/flat)
   - Deal expansion history
   - Cross-sell product adoption

5. CHURN RISK INVERSE (15% weight):
   - Inverse of churn probability signals
   - Factors: tenure, margin erosion speed, usage decline rate, disposition recency
   - Higher score = lower churn risk

Score Interpretation:
- 80-100: Healthy — maintain engagement cadence
- 60-79: Stable — monitor for trend changes
- 40-59: Watch — proactive outreach recommended
- 20-39: At Risk — intervention required within 7 days
- 0-19: Critical — immediate escalation to CS Manager

chunks.title: Two-Track Margin Thresholds
chunks.content:
Margin health categories differ by customer type (critical for accurate classification):

TIERED/FLAT RATE CUSTOMERS (ct != 'credit'):
- Negative: < 0% GM — actively losing money on this customer
- Critical: 0-20% GM — below minimum viable margin
- Watch: 20-40% GM — below target but sustainable short-term
- Healthy: 40%+ GM — at or above target margins

CREDIT/TRANSACTIONAL CUSTOMERS (ct == 'credit'):
- Negative: < 0% GM — actively losing money
- Critical: 0-15% GM — below minimum viable margin
- Watch: 16-26% GM — approaching healthy but needs monitoring
- Healthy: 27%+ GM — sustainable margin for credit model

Why Two Tracks: Credit customers operate on thinner margins by nature (per-pull pricing vs subscription). Applying tiered thresholds to credit customers would classify nearly all as "critical" — creating false alarm fatigue. The two-track system ensures CS teams focus on genuinely at-risk accounts.

Margin Velocity: Beyond the current margin %, the system tracks margin slope (mslp) — the trend direction over recent months. A customer at 35% GM but declining at -2% per month is higher priority than one at 25% GM that's been stable for 6 months.

dashboard_navigation.label: Dashboard Navigation
dashboard_navigation.description: CI Dashboard tabs, workflows, Chuck Outreach actions

chunks.title: CI Dashboard Tab Reference
chunks.content:
Customer Intelligence Dashboard — 10+ tabs for portfolio management:

1. PORTFOLIO OVERVIEW: KPI strip (total customers, avg health score, negative margin count, avg margin %), tier distribution chart, group health section. Quick-filter presets: Negative Margin, Credit Below 20%, Billing Lag, Watch List.

2. CUSTOMER HEALTH TABLE: Full customer list with sortable columns. Search, filter by tier/status/assignment. Click any row to open detail panel. Health score color coding. Disposition status indicators.

3. CUSTOMER DETAIL PANEL (slide-in): T12 financials (revenue, overages, COGS, GM%), bureau pull history, margin trend sparkline, SmartPull data, contact info, disposition form, Chuck Outreach actions.

4. REVENUE TIER ANALYSIS: Elite ($5K+ MRR), Tier 1 ($2K-$5K), Tier 2 ($800-$2K), Tier 3 (<$800). Distribution charts, tier-specific KPIs, engagement cadence tracking.

5. CROSS-SELL INTELLIGENCE: Customers with expansion opportunity. Current product portfolio vs potential additions. ICP scoring for upsell likelihood.

6. BUREAU OPTIMIZATION: Bureau pull patterns per customer. 3-bureau vs 2-bureau analysis. SmartPull adoption rates. Cost savings opportunities.

7. GUARDRAILS IMPACT: Guardrail savings calculations (overage prevention, usage optimization). CPQ allotment tracking.

8. CPQ TREND ALERTS: Quarterly CPQ utilization analysis. Risk scoring (Critical ≥75, High ≥55, Moderate ≥38). EOQ projections. Overage forecasting.

9. GM TRENDING: Margin trend analysis across portfolio. Top improvers and decliners. Velocity indicators.

10. DISPOSITION TRACKER: Full disposition history per customer. Status categories, contact outcomes, pipeline progress.

11. MY CUSTOMERS: Assigned portfolio with priority queue. Today's priorities, overdue touchpoints, full portfolio table.

12. CS MANAGEMENT (PIN protected): Team performance, coverage heatmap, unassigned accounts, assignment validation.

chunks.title: Chuck Outreach Actions (CI Dashboard)
chunks.content:
Chuck Outreach is an AI-powered tool embedded in the CI Dashboard customer detail panel. Four actions available:

1. RETENTION EMAIL (cs_health_check mode):
Generates a personalized retention/check-in email using customer's health score, margin, tenure, and package data. Includes subject line and body, ready to copy-paste. Best used for: regular touchpoint emails, re-engagement after silence, health score decline follow-up.

2. ACCOUNT REVIEW (cs_account_review mode):
Builds structured QBR/call prep talking points: account snapshot, key discussion topics, trends to highlight, risk flags, and recommended next steps. Best used for: quarterly business reviews, account review prep, manager 1:1 prep.

3. REPRICE PROPOSAL (cs_reprice_scenario mode):
Produces a data-driven reprice analysis: current pricing state, justification for repricing (margin erosion, bureau overusage, tier misalignment), recommended adjustment, and customer talking points. Best used for: margin recovery conversations, tier migration proposals, renewal pricing reviews.

4. UTILIZATION REVIEW (cs_utilization_guidance mode):
Drafts a bureau usage alignment review: current pull patterns, where usage doesn't match tier/pricing, margin impact, and a short outreach script. Best used for: CPQ overage prevention, bureau optimization conversations, usage alignment reviews.

Each action sends real customer data (MCID, group, package, tier, health score, margin, MRR, tenure, bureau averages, root causes) to the AI for personalized responses.

disposition_taxonomy.label: Disposition Taxonomy
disposition_taxonomy.description: Disposition types, classification rules, best practices

chunks.title: Disposition Categories & Classification
chunks.content:
Disposition taxonomy used across CI Dashboard, PIE, and Chuck:

CONTACT OUTCOMES:
- Contacted - Interested: Customer engaged, expressed interest in discussion
- Contacted - Not Interested: Customer engaged but declined
- Contacted - Left Message: Voicemail or message left, awaiting callback
- Contacted - Wrong Contact: Reached wrong person, need updated contact info
- No Answer: Call attempted, no response
- Email Sent: Outreach via email, awaiting response

PIPELINE PROGRESS:
- Meeting Scheduled: QBR, review, or discussion meeting confirmed
- Meeting Completed: Meeting occurred, follow-up actions identified
- Proposal Sent: Pricing proposal, reprice document, or tier migration proposal delivered
- Negotiating: Active pricing or contract negotiation underway
- Opportunity Created: Formal Salesforce opportunity generated (cross-sell or upsell)

CLOSED:
- Closed Won: Deal completed, new product/tier activated
- Closed Lost: Customer declined, reason documented
- Cancelled: Customer requested cancellation (escalate to CS Manager immediately)

DATA QUALITY:
- Bad Data: Customer record has incorrect information (MRR, contact, package)
- Duplicate: Record is a duplicate of another customer entry
- Not a Customer: Record should not be in the active customer database

CS Best Practice: Log every customer interaction as a disposition. Frequency of dispositions feeds into priority scoring and contact cadence tracking. No disposition in 14+ days = flagged as "Needs Attention" in the priority queue.

cogs_structure.label: COGS Structure
cogs_structure.description: Cost of goods sold calculation, billable code mapping

chunks.title: COGS Calculation & Billable Code Mapping
chunks.content:
COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) Structure:

Total Monthly COGS = Bureau Pull Costs + Product COGS + Subcode Fees

Bureau Pull Costs: Each credit bureau pull has a per-pull cost. Costs vary by bureau and pull type (hard vs soft). See Bureau Cost Structure chunk for current rates.

Billable Code Categories (from Snowflake FACT_SUB_PRODUCT_COGS_V2):

Equifax: EQSP (Soft Pull), EQS (Score), EQHP (Hard Pull), EQHPS (Hard Pull Score), EQPQ (Prequalification), EQ3B (3-Bureau)
Experian: XPPS (Prescreen), XPSP (Soft Pull), XPSS (Score), XPHP (Hard Pull), XPHPS (Hard Pull Score), XPPQ (Prequalification), XP3B (3-Bureau)
TransUnion: TUSP (Soft Pull), TUSS (Score), TUHP (Hard Pull), TUHPS (Hard Pull Score), TUPQ (Prequalification), TU3B (3-Bureau)
Other: IDVP/IDVS/IDVE (ID Verify), OFAC (OFAC Check), COMP (Compliance), CDPS/CDHS (CBC+DSGSS pulls)

Margin Formula:
- Gross Margin $ = Net MRR - Total COGS
- Gross Margin % = (Net MRR - Total COGS) / Net MRR × 100

Common Margin Erosion Causes:
- Bureau cost increases (upstream from Equifax/Experian/TransUnion)
- Customer over-pulling beyond allotment (tiered customers)
- Price not keeping pace with COGS inflation
- Product mix shift (adding lower-margin products)

chunks.title: Product Escalation SOP
chunks.content:
Product Escalation Standard Operating Procedure:

Before Escalating to Product:
- Confirm issue reviewed by another support team member (second-level assessment)
- For Credit Solution (CDC) issues: Get second opinion from Vicky
- For Compliance issues: Get second opinion from Cory

Required Escalation Template (EVERY escalation must include):
1. Dealership name(s) and account number(s)
2. Date reported
3. Specific product or system impacted
4. Problem reported (what is happening?)
5. Expected Outcome (what is expected behavior?)
6. Steps to replicate the issue
7. Screenshots with comments (if possible)
8. Additional context for investigation

Why Complete Info Matters:
- Protects Product team's time (they handle high-value complex issues)
- Prevents back-and-forth between teams
- Ensures proper qualification (not every issue belongs with Product)
- Reduces resolution time

Impact-Urgency-Priority Matrix:
- P1 (Critical): Total system outage, all users impacted, no workaround. Response: Immediate.
- P2 (High): Major feature broken, many users impacted, limited workaround. Response: 4 hours.
- P3 (Medium): Feature degraded, some users impacted, workaround available. Response: 1 business day.
- P4 (Low): Minor issue, few users, easy workaround or enhancement request. Response: 3 business days.

support_talk_tracks.label: Support Talk Tracks
support_talk_tracks.description: Customer-facing talk tracks for common scenarios

chunks.title: Hard Pull Limit Talk Tracks
chunks.content:
Support Team Talk Tracks — 1 Hard Pull Limit (Total Solution/Control packages):

When dealer asks "Why can't I run another hard pull?":
"Our system now limits each customer to one hard credit pull within a 30-day window. This is part of our Smart Credit Subscription Management under the Total Solution package. The goal is to protect the customer's credit, avoid duplicate charges, and keep your dealership within monthly subscription limits."

When dealer says "But I need to run another one!":
"If a hard pull has been completed in the last 30 days, the system shows the existing report instead of allowing another pull. There is no override — it's fully enforced to maintain credit usage fairness and compliance."

When asked "What happens when I click OK on the message?":
"Clicking OK displays the most recent hard credit report already run on the customer. No new report is ordered and your subscription is not charged again."

When asked "When can I run another hard pull?":
"You can run another hard pull once 30 days have passed since the last one. The system automatically detects when the window resets."

When asked "Is there anything else I can do?":
"Yes — your team can still run additional soft pulls using other bureaus to prequalify the customer or check updated credit data. This gives you flexibility without affecting the hard pull limit."

When a lender requests another hard pull:
"The 30-day limit applies to all pulls within our system. If a lender specifically requires a new report from a different bureau, contact your Informativ CS representative to discuss options."

Key CS Principle: The hard pull limit is a FEATURE, not a limitation. Frame it as protecting the dealer's subscription value and their customer's credit.

integration_workflows.label: Integration Workflows
integration_workflows.description: CRM, DMS, and digital retail integration SOPs

chunks.title: VinSolutions CRM Integration Setup
chunks.content:
VinSolutions CRM API Integration — Step-by-Step Setup:

Prerequisites: Active Informativ customer, VinSolutions CRM, dealer provides CRM ID.

Client Responsibilities:
1. Provide correct CRM ID
2. Enable Informativ integration inside VinSolutions
3. Configure Lead Sources: "Informativ-Internet" (web app leads) and "Informativ-Showroom" (DL scans, PreQuals)

Informativ Support Responsibilities:
1. If existing CBC client: Disable CBC ADF/XML Feed to prevent duplicates
2. In Compliance Admin Portal (admin.dsgss.com): Set CRM Type to VinSolutions, enter CRM ID
3. If existing customer: Add CRM Last Customer ID (omitting pushes all historical deal jackets)
4. Remove CRM Email Field Value (must be blank — Vin API won't enable with value)
5. Save → Click "Generate User ID" button → Save again

Verification: Submit test credit pull, verify lead appears in VinSolutions. Check buyer and co-buyer data populates correctly.

chunks.title: CarNow Integration Setup
chunks.content:
CarNow/Informativ Credit Integration SOP:

Purpose: Enable credit integration between CarNow digital retail and Informativ Credit platform.

Process:
Step 1 — Request Initiation: CarNow submits integration request for shared client
Step 2 — Client Support Initial Processing: Manage intake, maintain consistency across teams
Step 3 — CarNow Confirmation: CarNow confirms readiness to support credit integration for shared clients

SLA: Standard integration setup timeline (refer to SLA documentation)

Key Guidelines:
- Integration creates Digital Deal Jacket (DDJ) on Informativ Compliance platform
- Soft pull ordered directly from Informativ Credit platform
- DDJ created automatically on Compliance platform with soft pull included
- Applies to all CarNow/Informativ shared dealer clients

csm_lifecycle.label: CSM: Credit Customer Lifecycle
csm_lifecycle.description: Credit customer lifecycle phases and risk management

chunks.title: Credit Customer Lifecycle & Risk Inflection Points
chunks.content:
Credit Customer Lifecycle Management (CSM Track):

PHASE 1 — ONBOARDING (Days 0-30):
- Bureau configuration and testing
- Initial pull volume baseline established
- Dealer training on portal access
- First invoice cycle — verify pricing accuracy
- Risk: Misconfigured bureau settings → overbilling → early churn signal

PHASE 2 — STABILIZATION (Days 31-90):
- Pull volume normalizes to dealer's actual activity
- First margin calculation becomes meaningful (need 2-3 months of data)
- Compare actual usage vs quoted volume
- Risk: Volume significantly below quote → dealer not adopting → investigate

PHASE 3 — STEADY STATE (Months 4-12):
- Predictable pull patterns established
- Margin tracked against 27% healthy threshold
- Bureau optimization opportunities identified
- Quarterly touch cadence for Tier 2, monthly for Tier 1
- Risk: Gradual volume decline → competitor evaluation or market conditions

PHASE 4 — RENEWAL/EXPANSION (Month 10+):
- T12 review shows full annual economics
- Cross-sell evaluation (add compliance, payments, SmartPencil)
- Tier migration candidate assessment
- Reprice if margin has eroded below threshold
- Risk: Renewal without addressing margin erosion locks in bad economics

CHURN SIGNALS (act within 7 days):
- Pull volume drops >20% month-over-month
- Health score drops below 40
- No response to 3+ outreach attempts
- Customer contacts support about cancellation
- Competitor name mentioned in any interaction

engagement_cadence.label: Engagement Cadence
engagement_cadence.description: Tier-based contact frequency and touchpoint requirements

chunks.title: Tier-Based Contact Cadence
chunks.content:
Revenue Tier Contact Cadence (CS operational standard):

ELITE ($500K+ ARR / $41,667+ MRR):
- Cadence: 30 days maximum between touchpoints
- Model: High-touch, strategic, regular business reviews
- Assigned: Named CSA or CSM (not pooled)
- Activities: Quarterly QBR, monthly check-in call, proactive optimization reviews
- Escalation: If no contact in 35 days → auto-flag to CS Manager

TIER 1 ($100K-$499K ARR / $8,333-$41,666 MRR):
- Cadence: 30 days
- Model: Monthly touchpoints, retention + expansion focus
- Assigned: Named CSA or CSM
- Activities: Semi-annual business review, monthly health check, cross-sell evaluation
- Escalation: If no contact in 35 days → auto-flag

TIER 2 ($20K-$99K ARR / $1,667-$8,332 MRR):
- Cadence: 60 days
- Model: Scaled engagement, CSM outreach + automated nudges
- Assigned: Pooled CS
- Activities: Annual review, quarterly automated check-in, usage alerts
- Escalation: If no contact in 70 days → auto-flag

TIER 3 (<$20K ARR / <$1,667 MRR):
- Cadence: 90 days
- Model: Automated + self-service, Informativ U training resources
- Assigned: Tech-touch (automated workflows)
- Activities: Automated usage reports, training nudges, annual review if flagged
- Escalation: If health score < 40 → route to live CS rep regardless of tier

Priority Override: Any customer with health score < 40 or negative margin overrides tier cadence and gets immediate attention regardless of ARR.

chunks.title: Reprice Communication Sequence
chunks.content:
14-Day Reprice Communication Sequence:

DAY 1 — INTERNAL PREP:
- Pull T12 financials from CI Dashboard detail panel
- Document current pricing, margin %, margin trend
- Calculate target pricing using reprice calculator (minimum GM% per tier floor)
- Generate Reprice Proposal via Chuck Outreach for talking points
- Get manager approval if repricing below manager threshold

DAY 3 — INITIAL OUTREACH:
- Email notification with context (not the new price yet)
- Frame: "As part of our regular account review, I'd like to discuss your current pricing and how we can ensure you're getting maximum value from your Informativ partnership."
- Goal: Set a call/meeting within the next week

DAY 5-7 — REPRICE CONVERSATION:
- Present T12 data: show the margin erosion trajectory
- Explain the WHY: bureau cost increases, usage patterns, feature adoption
- Present the adjustment with value framing (not just "price increase")
- Anchor on tier value: what they get at each level
- If pushback: use manager price as negotiation lever (between target and floor)

DAY 10 — FOLLOW-UP:
- If no response: second outreach attempt
- If negotiating: present options (tier adjustment, usage optimization, pricing middle ground)
- Document all touchpoints as dispositions in CI Dashboard

DAY 14 — RESOLUTION:
- Close the reprice action (accepted, modified, declined)
- If declined: escalate to CS Manager with full history
- If accepted: coordinate with billing/ops for pricing change implementation
- Update disposition with outcome

GUARDRAILS:
- Never quote below tier floor (Protect 39.5%, Enrich 44.5%, Elevate 52%, Control 42%)
- Cross-sell MRR indicator: never quote below customer's current MRR
- Document every step for audit trail

chunks.title: Reprice Negotiation Guardrails
chunks.content:
Reprice Negotiation Authority & Guardrails:

AUTHORITY LEVELS:
- CS Rep: Can propose repricing at or above Target GM% for the tier
- CS Manager: Can approve repricing between Target and Manager Threshold
- Deal Desk/CRO: Required for any pricing below Manager Threshold
- Absolute Floor: No deal priced below tier-specific floor under any circumstance

NEGOTIATION LEVERS (in order of preference):
1. VALUE REFRAME: "Your current package includes [features]. At the proposed pricing, you're still getting [X]% more value than a standalone credit solution."
2. USAGE OPTIMIZATION: "I can help you reduce your per-pull costs by optimizing your bureau configuration, which offsets the pricing adjustment."
3. TIER MIGRATION: "If the current tier pricing doesn't work, we can look at a different tier that better matches your volume."
4. MANAGER PRICE: Between target and floor — use sparingly, requires manager approval
5. TERM COMMITMENT: Offer pricing stability (12-month lock) in exchange for accepting the adjustment

WHAT TO AVOID:
- Never lead with the lowest price you can offer
- Never promise pricing you don't have authority to deliver
- Never reprice without data (always have T12 financials ready)
- Never reprice reactive to a cancellation threat without CS Manager involvement
- Never match competitor pricing without Deal Desk approval

csa_onboarding.label: CSA: Onboarding & Installation
csa_onboarding.description: Tier customer onboarding checklist and go-live validation

chunks.title: Tier Customer Installation Checklist
chunks.content:
Tier Customer Onboarding & Installation Checklist (CSA Track):

PRE-INSTALLATION (Before Go-Live):
□ Bureau configuration verified (EQ/EX/TU settings match contract)
□ SmartPull settings configured (if included in tier)
□ Guardrail rules set (hard pull limits, soft pull routing)
□ DMS integration enabled (DealerTrack, RouteOne, CDK, etc.)
□ CRM integration configured (VinSolutions, eLeads, Tekion, etc.)
□ Test pulls completed successfully (1 soft, 1 hard per bureau)
□ Compliance workflows activated (DL scan, e-signatures, adverse action)
□ User accounts created for dealership staff

GO-LIVE (Day 1):
□ First production pull confirmed successful
□ DDJ (Digital Deal Jacket) creation verified
□ CRM lead push verified (if integrated)
□ DMS data push verified (if integrated)
□ Dealer staff trained on portal access
□ SmartPencil demo completed (if Control tier)
□ Support contact information provided

FIRST VALUE FRAMEWORK (Day 7):
□ First week pull volume reviewed vs expected
□ Any configuration issues resolved
□ Dealer feedback collected (what's working, what's not)
□ Follow-up training scheduled if adoption is low
□ Escalate to CS Manager if zero pulls in first 7 days

30-DAY CHECKPOINT:
□ Pull volume meets 80%+ of expected baseline
□ All integrations functioning without errors
□ Dealer satisfaction confirmed (NPS or verbal)
□ CPQ allotment tracking verified
□ First month margin calculation reviewed

utilization_playbook.label: CSA: Utilization Management
utilization_playbook.description: CPQ utilization monitoring and intervention playbooks

chunks.title: CPQ Utilization Management
chunks.content:
Utilization Management Playbook (CSA Track):

UNDER-UTILIZATION (<50% of CPQ allotment):
Signals: Dealer not fully using subscribed services. Revenue at risk — dealer may downgrade or churn.

Investigation Steps:
1. Check bureau pull history in CI Dashboard detail panel
2. Compare current month pulls vs CPQ allotment (spc/hpc × 3 for quarterly)
3. Review if specific bureaus are underused
4. Check if SmartPull is routing effectively

Intervention Actions:
- Schedule training refresher on underused features
- Demonstrate value of unused capabilities (compliance features, SmartPencil)
- Review if dealer staff turnover caused knowledge loss
- Consider temporary usage incentive if churn risk is high
- Talk track: "I noticed your team is only using about [X]% of your included services. Let me show you some features that could help your team close more deals."

OVER-UTILIZATION (>100% of CPQ allotment):
Signals: Overage charges accumulating. Dealer may be upset by unexpected costs. Could also signal business growth (positive).

Investigation Steps:
1. Check if overage is one-time spike or sustained trend
2. Review bureau efficiency (are they over-pulling unnecessarily?)
3. Calculate overage cost impact on margin

Intervention Actions:
- If sustained growth: Propose tier upgrade (more allotment at better per-pull economics)
- If inefficient pulling: Optimize bureau configuration (reduce 3-bureau to 2-bureau)
- If temporary spike: Explain overage billing and monitor next month
- Talk track: "Great news — your team's usage shows strong business activity. Let me review your plan to make sure you're getting the best value for your volume."

CPQ RISK SCORING (from CI Dashboard CPQ Trend Alerts tab):
- Critical (≥75): Immediate intervention — likely overage or severe underuse
- High (≥55): Schedule review within 1 week
- Moderate (≥38): Monitor — include in next scheduled touchpoint
- Low (<38): No action needed this cycle
chunks.source: CS Operations Plan + app.js CPQ logic

chunks.title: At-Risk Classification & Save Strategies
chunks.content:
Retention Framework — At-Risk Customer Management:

AT-RISK CLASSIFICATION:
1. Health Score < 40 (any tier, any customer type)
2. Negative margin for 2+ consecutive months
3. Pull volume decline >20% MoM for 2+ months
4. No disposition logged in 2× tier cadence (e.g., 60 days for Tier 1)
5. Customer mentioned cancellation or competitor in any interaction
6. Failed to respond to 3+ consecutive outreach attempts

SAVE STRATEGIES (by risk type):

MARGIN EROSION:
- Immediate: Run reprice analysis via Chuck Outreach
- Short-term: Optimize bureau configuration to reduce COGS
- Medium-term: Propose tier migration if current tier economics are broken
- Last resort: Manager-price offer with term commitment

USAGE DECLINE:
- Immediate: Check for staff turnover at dealership (common cause)
- Short-term: Schedule re-training on key features
- Medium-term: Demonstrate new capabilities (SmartPencil, guardrails)
- Last resort: Temporary usage incentive (waive overages for 1 month)

COMPETITOR THREAT:
- Immediate: Log competitive intel in disposition notes
- Short-term: Pull competitive battlecard (700Credit, NCC comparison points)
- Medium-term: Demonstrate integration depth + switching costs
- Escalation: If deal > $50K ARR, involve Sales Director

CANCELLATION REQUEST:
- IMMEDIATE: Escalate to CS Manager (do not process without manager review)
- Gather: cancellation reason, competitor name, timeline
- Counter: T12 value summary, switching costs, contract terms
- Decision: CS Manager determines if save offer is warranted based on LTV

cross_sell.label: Cross-Sell Identification
cross_sell.description: Cross-sell signals, paths, and sales handoff protocol

chunks.title: Cross-Sell Signal Identification
chunks.content:
Cross-Sell Opportunity Identification:

HIGH-PROBABILITY SIGNALS:
1. Health Score > 70 (stable, engaged customer — receptive to expansion)
2. Utilization at 60-80% of allotment (using the product well but not maxed)
3. Competitor product detected in integration stack (e.g., 700Credit for credit, but no compliance)
4. Recently completed successful reprice (demonstrated willingness to invest)
5. New DMS or CRM integration activated (technology modernization = buying mode)

CROSS-SELL PATHS:
- Credit-only → Add Compliance (DSGSS): Frame as risk reduction, FTC compliance
- Credit-only → Add Payments (SmartPencil): Frame as deal speed and profit optimization
- Compliance-only → Add Credit: Frame as integrated workflow, eliminate dual-vendor complexity
- Protect tier → Upgrade to Control: Frame as credit controls + customer insights
- Any tier → Add SmartPencil: Frame as credit-qualified payments = faster closes

HANDOFF TO SALES:
- CS identifies the opportunity and qualifies interest
- CS documents: customer data, expressed interest, recommended product, estimated volume
- CS creates Salesforce opportunity OR notifies assigned Sales rep
- Revenue credit model: CS gets retention credit, Sales gets new business credit
- CS remains customer relationship owner through and after the sale

QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (before handoff):
- Customer has expressed interest (not just CS assumption)
- Current account is healthy (HS > 60, no active margin issues)
- No open support escalations
- Contact is a decision-maker or can introduce one

chunks.title: 700Credit vs Informativ Positioning
chunks.content:
700Credit Competitive Comparison (for CS retention conversations):

WHERE 700Credit COMPETES:
- Primary: Credit bureau access (hard/soft pulls)
- Secondary: Basic compliance features
- Geography: National presence, similar dealer base

INFORMATIV ADVANTAGES (retention talking points):
1. INTEGRATED PLATFORM: Informativ combines Credit + Compliance + Payments + SmartPencil in one platform. 700Credit requires separate vendors for compliance and payments.
2. SMARTPENCIL: No equivalent product at 700Credit. Credit-qualified payments is a unique differentiator.
3. GUARDRAILS & CONTROLS: Control tier offers automated credit usage management. 700Credit has no comparable feature.
4. COMPLIANCE DEPTH: DSGSS provides full digital deal jacket, e-signatures, adverse action automation, 5-year storage. 700Credit's compliance is basic.
5. TOTAL COST OF OWNERSHIP: Multiple point solutions from 700Credit + compliance vendor + payments vendor > Informativ Total Solution pricing.

WHEN A CUSTOMER MENTIONS 700Credit:
- Don't panic — it's often a pricing lever, not a real threat
- Ask: "What specifically are they offering that's attractive?"
- Listen for: price (can counter with total solution value), feature (likely SmartPencil gap), relationship (harder to counter)
- Quantify switching costs: re-integration with DMS/CRM, retraining staff, compliance gap during transition
- Pull T12 data to show value delivered over the partnership

NCC COMPETITIVE NOTES:
- Smaller player, regional presence
- Primarily credit-focused, minimal compliance
- Often competes on price alone
- Counter: Compliance risk exposure without DSGSS, no payment technology

compliance_knowledge.label: Compliance Knowledge
compliance_knowledge.description: DSGSS platform overview, FTC Safeguards, compliance workflows

chunks.title: DSGSS Compliance Platform Overview
chunks.content:
DSGSS (Dealer Safeguard Systems) Compliance Platform:

Core Problem: Compliance is not optional, training is temporary, turnover is consistent, fraud is increasing. Virtual/remote deals add complexity — sources now include walk-in, web applications, and digital retailing across 3+ different platforms. The necessary compliance steps are consistent regardless of where engagement starts.

Compliance Risk Areas by Sales Stage:
1. MEET & GREET: Driver's license and insurance card collection, data safeguarding
2. CREDIT APPLICATION: Permissible purpose, privacy notice, authorization
3. CREDIT PULL: Red Flag alerts, OOW (Out of Wallet) questions, fraud detection
4. DEAL STRUCTURING: Risk-based pricing notices, adverse action compliance
5. DOCUMENT COLLECTION: Stips verification, digital deal jacket, e-signatures
6. STORAGE: 5-year dead deal jacket retention, encrypted storage

Key Compliance Features:
- Red Flag hard stop alerts (cannot proceed without addressing)
- Driver's license scan with barcode verification (250+ formats)
- Synthetic fraud detection with out-of-wallet questions
- Automated adverse action letters (electronic + mail house)
- Risk-based pricing notices
- OFAC check integration
- Multi-rooftop compliance tracking dashboard
- Real-time compliance alerts and notifications

FTC Safeguards Rule: Informativ's platform helps dealers comply with the FTC Safeguards Rule by providing automated, consistent workflows that protect customer data at every stage of the sales process.

CS Relevance: Compliance features are a key retention tool — switching away from Informativ means losing automated compliance, which exposes the dealer to regulatory risk.

partnership_rules.label: Partnership Rules
partnership_rules.description: CUDL partnership SLAs and engagement rules

chunks.title: CUDL Partnership Rules of Engagement
chunks.content:
Informativ-CUDL Partnership Rules of Engagement:

Lead Definition: A prospect contacted who has expressed interest in Informativ/CUDL services.

Lead Attribution:
- Referring party documents leads via webform submission
- RevOps validates if prospect is current client
- If current client on Total Solution/Control/Protect: lead disqualified (no cross-sell potential)
- If prospect OR client not on flat rate: RevOps evaluates existing opportunities

Opportunity Evaluation:
- Existing opportunity with verified activity in last 30 days: stays with managing company
- Automated mass communications do NOT qualify as verified activity
- No existing opportunity: lead attributed to referring party

Follow-up SLA Requirements (Critical for CS):
- Initial Acknowledgment: 4 hours (or 9am next business day)
- First Contact Attempt: 12 hours (or 12pm next business day)
- Second Contact Attempt: 24 hours
- Required Outcome (solution fit or demo): 48 hours
- Escalation: 24h flag, 48h manager notification, reassignment at 48h

SLA Compliance Indicators:
- Green: Has solution fit meeting OR (qualified/working + has activity)
- Yellow: Within first 24hrs no activity OR activity but no outcome within 48hrs
- Red: >48hrs no solution fit meeting OR >24hrs zero activity

Commission & Quota:
- 50/50 split between RSM and affiliate
- Separate CUDL quota with 50/50 split
- 6-month attribution window
- Post-installation: affiliate owns account

payments_knowledge.label: Payments Knowledge
payments_knowledge.description: Dealer payment glossary and SmartPencil context

chunks.title: Dealer Payment Glossary (Key Terms)
chunks.content:
Essential Payment Terms for CS Conversations:

MSRP: Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price. Used for lease residuals, incentive amounts. Displayed on Monroney sticker.

Sales Price: Final negotiated price before taxes/fees. Determines loan amount or capitalized cost for leasing.

Bookout Value: Market/lender-recognized value (NADA, KBB, Black Book). Lenders limit advances based on this. Critical for LTV calculations.

Money Factor: Decimal representation of lease interest rate. Multiply by 2,400 for approximate APR (e.g., 0.00125 × 2400 = 3.0% APR).

Residual Value: Projected value at lease end as % of MSRP. Higher residual = lower monthly payment. Set by OEM/lender, not negotiable.

Cap Cost Reduction: Down payment on a lease. Reduces capitalized cost → lower payment.

Negative Equity: When trade-in value < remaining loan balance. Can be rolled into new loan (increases LTV and payment).

LTV (Loan-to-Value): Loan amount ÷ vehicle value. Lenders cap LTV (typically 120-140%). Higher LTV = higher risk = higher rate.

Back-End Gross: Profit from F&I products (warranties, GAP, paint protection). Separate from front-end (vehicle) profit.

SmartPencil Relevance: SmartPencil uses these variables to calculate real, credit-qualified payments. CS teams should understand these terms when discussing SmartPencil value with dealers — especially Money Factor, Residual, and LTV as they directly impact payment accuracy.

digital_retail_integrations.label: Digital Retail Integrations
digital_retail_integrations.description: Digital retail platform landscape and integration status

chunks.title: Digital Retail Platform Landscape
chunks.content:
Digital Retail Platform Integration Landscape (2024-2025):

Major Platforms & Informativ Integration Status:
- AutoFi (~10,000 dealers): Credit Integration ACTIVE
- Gubagoo (~8,500 dealers): Leads Integration Q3 2025
- Darwin/J.D. Power (~7,700 dealers): No current integration
- Cox "Accelerate My Deal" (5,000+ dealers): No current integration
- CDK/Roadster (~3,000 dealers): No current integration
- CarNow (thousands, multiple OEM programs): Credit Integration IN PROGRESS
- Upstart Auto Retail (>500 dealers): Leads Integration Q3 2025
- Tekion (few hundred, large groups): No current integration

Integration Types:
1. Leads Integration: Platform creates Digital Deal Jacket (DDJ) on Informativ Compliance by passing customer data (name, address) via Informativ Leads API
2. Credit Integration: Platform creates DDJ by ordering Soft Pull directly from Informativ Credit. DDJ created automatically on Compliance platform with soft pull included.

CS Relevance: When customers mention digital retail platforms, know which ones integrate with Informativ. Integration depth is a retention argument — "your digital retail workflow is connected to our compliance platform, switching credit providers would break that."

CRM Integrations: VinSolutions (lead + DL scan push), eLeads (CBC web app/portal), Tekion (document sending), DriveCentric (lead + DL scan), DealerSocket (SocketCredit exclusive to CBC)

DMS Integrations: DealerTrack (CBC push + DSGSS updates), RouteOne (data push + DSGSS), DealerBuilt/LightYear (custom), Advent, PBS, Quorum, AutoManager
chunks.source: Digital Retail Platform Adoption.docx + Integrations KB

cs_assignment_model.label: CS Assignment Model
cs_assignment_model.description: CSA vs CSM routing, team roster, priority scoring

chunks.title: CSA vs CSM Assignment Routing
chunks.content:
CS Team Assignment Model:

CLIENT SUCCESS ADVISORS (CSA) — Flat Rate + Compliance Clients:
- Chad Manz
- Chaquowa Bouvier
- Kedrick Butler
- Irma Schweitzer
- Ruby Gomez
Focus: Tier installation, adoption, utilization optimization, renewal management

CLIENT SUCCESS MANAGERS (CSM) — Credit Solutions / Transactional Clients:
- Esther Aikey
- Kelly Felder
- Open Role (all affiliate accounts assigned here until filled)
Focus: Margin management, bureau optimization, reprice execution, credit lifecycle

CS MANAGER: Kiristen Sandoval
Focus: Team oversight, escalation handling, strategic account decisions

Assignment Validation:
- CSA assigned to credit customer → Amber warning (type mismatch)
- CSM assigned to tiered customer → Amber warning (type mismatch)
- Warnings are informational — override allowed for special circumstances
- CS Management tab in CI Dashboard shows assignment split and mismatch count

Priority Scoring (0-100) drives daily work queue:
- Health Velocity: 30% (rate of health score change)
- Margin Erosion: 25% (speed of margin decline)
- Renewal Proximity: 20% (days until contract renewal)
- Contact Recency: 15% (days since last disposition)
- Revenue at Risk: 10% (MRR of the account)

category_metadata.name: Email Drafting Standards
category_metadata.purpose: Marketing-approved standards for every customer-facing email Chuck generates.

chunks.title: Email Drafting Standards — Marketing-Approved (Christina Wofford, April 2026)
chunks.content:
Marketing reviewed Chuck-drafted CS outreach in April 2026 and flagged systemic issues. These rules govern every CUSTOMER-FACING email Chuck generates. Internal coaching conversations are unaffected.

SUBJECT LINE STANDARDS
- Default check-in: 'Checking in: [Account Name] & Informativ'
- Account at risk / intervention: 'Let's ensure the success of [Account Name] with Informativ'
- Always fill in the actual account name. Never leave brackets, [X], or Q[X] unfilled.
- Do NOT generate creative or quirky subject lines (e.g., 'Quick Check-In on...', 'Let's Align on..., — Q[X] Review').

REQUIRED OPENER
Every customer-facing email opens with a thank-you for the partnership BEFORE any other content. Suggested wording: 'Thank you for your partnership! I'm reaching out to check in on your experience with our solutions. We value your feedback and want to ensure everything is meeting your expectations. From our reporting, I can see that your account is performing well overall, and am noticing some areas where we may be able to deliver even more value.'

HARD PROHIBITIONS
1. NEVER mention margin in any customer-facing email. Margin is internal-only. This includes 'margin %', 'gross margin', 'profitability', 'margin performance', 'margin headwinds'.
2. NEVER say 'everyone wins' — implies Informativ wins something from the customer. Use 'we want to make sure you're getting the most value' instead.
3. Do NOT include tier names (Tier I, Tier II, Tier III, Protect Tier, Enrich Tier, Elevate Tier, Control Tier, Foundation). Tier nomenclature is internal-only.
4. Do NOT name specific products the customer 'has' or 'is using' — Marketing's spot-checks find these are frequently inaccurate. Use neutral capability language ('your credit and compliance workflow', 'your current setup') instead of naming SmartPencil, guardrails, Customer Insights, etc.
5. Do NOT include package names by default. If unavoidable, the correct form is 'Total Solution' (singular). NEVER 'Total Solutions' (plural).
6. Standard meeting ask is 20 minutes. Don't switch between 15/20/30 minutes across emails.

STANDARD CTA (use verbatim or as close as possible)
'I'd love to schedule a brief, 20-minute call in the next two weeks to walk through these together. This will help ensure we're supporting you in the way that works best for your team. Please use the link to my calendar to schedule a time that works best for you, or just reply to this email.'

The phrase 'the link to my calendar' is intentional — the rep's email signature carries the actual booking link. Do NOT generate a placeholder URL or {{booking_link}} token.

LANGUAGE REWRITES
- 'Upcoming priorities' → 'What initiatives or plans for the next quarter where Informativ may be able to assist'
- 'Over the past few months, we've seen some headwinds on margin performance' → REWRITE — strip margin entirely. Use 'I'd like to walk through your current credit pull patterns and identify any quick wins we can unlock together' instead.

FORMATTING
- Two blank lines between paragraphs
- One blank line between bullet points
- Uniform spacing throughout — no mixed spacing
- Structure: Subject → '---' separator → Greeting → Partnership opener → 2-3 short body paragraphs → 3 bulleted discussion topics (max 4) → Standard CTA → Sign-off (Name, Title, Informativ)

TONE
Warm but professional. Trusted advisor / partner — never vendor or salesperson. Lead with customer outcomes (efficiency, value, smoother workflow). Avoid internal-sounding language, jargon, and technical terms unless the customer raised them first.

VALIDATION CHECKLIST
Before returning any customer-facing email, verify:
1. Subject follows 'Checking in: [Account] & Informativ' or 'Let's ensure the success of [Account] with Informativ' — no creative variants
2. Subject has no unfilled brackets or Q[X]
3. Email opens with thank-you for partnership BEFORE any other content
4. ZERO mentions of margin (any variant)
5. ZERO tier names
6. ZERO 'Total Solutions' (plural). Only 'Total Solution' (singular) if package mentioned
7. ZERO specific product names attributed to customer
8. ZERO 'everyone wins' or close paraphrase
9. Standard 20-minute CTA with 'link to my calendar' phrasing
10. Uniform 2-line paragraph spacing, 1-line bullet spacing
11. Sign-off includes rep name, title, and 'Informativ' on its own line

If ANY checklist item fails, rewrite the email. Marketing has flagged that prior Chuck output failed multiple checks at once.
